blocks.text: Laser Lens Tek, Inc. (DBA American Photonics), hereinafter referred to as APC or the Company,
is a manufacturer of high-precision optical components, film coatings, and other infrared
CO2 high power, low power, and fiber laser
APC primarily serves the US market, with more than 90% of its business coming from the
industrial, medical, manufacturing, aerospace, and military industries. The remaining
estimated 10% of revenue comes from international sales. Management notes that APC's total
sales are divided into 20% online sales and 80% corporate sales, approximately. Within the
corporate sales segment, APC primarily serves the industrial, medical, manufacturing,
aerospace, and military markets. In the online sales segment, APC caters to engravers and
The Company operates from an 18,000 sq. ft. facility leased from a related entity. Management
notes that the real estate may be leased long-term.
The Company is wholly owned by a related entity, which is owned equally by a husband-and-
wife team. Both owners would like to start exploring retirement options and join with a partner
who can capitalize on the many opportunities APC is offering. Both owners are willing to remain
with the Company for a short period to ensure a smooth transition under new ownership, with
management noting that one of the owners is open to remaining with the Company long-term.
From 2021 to 2024, gross profit and adjusted EBITDA margins averaged 67.4% and 7.9%,
Diverse product offerings, with no one segment representing more than 40% of overall
As of January 2025, the Company has over $270k remaining to be billed on current orders.

Global distribution network with a presence in over 85 countries.

APC is a manufacturer of high-precision optical components, providing a wide range of
products for both low-power and high-power CO2, infrared, and custom optics.
Products are typically sold to end-users, original equipment manufacturers (OEMs), and
through a distribution network covering over 85 countries.
The Company operates from a world-class facility, consisting of state-of-the-art
manufacturing equipment, laboratories, processing chambers, and office space.
Management notes that with its first major prime military contractor secured and a multi-
year ZnSe supply contract in place, APC is now positioned for aggressive revenue growth
in military, aerospace, and global industrial markets.
The contract acts as an entry for APC into the prime contractor ecosystem for future
Management notes that, in response to evolving market demands, APC has aggressively
diversified its product portfolio, launching over 20 new products from 2020 to 2024 in the
low-power CO2 laser segment and custom optics solutions.
Management notes that the Company faced a significant negative financial impact from
the COVID-19 pandemic, particularly caused by delays in raw material shipments from
trade restrictions, halted production, and decreased demand from delayed orders and
Raw materials, such as Germanium (Ge) had import restrictions from China, while
Gallium Arsenide (GaAs) faced an import ban from China to the US.
The Company experienced reduced demand for high-power CO  laser lens
replacement parts, affecting traditional revenue streams.
Management notes that, as a result of restrictions surrounding certain raw materials
and reduced demand for specific products, the Company made a strategic shift
away from high and pow power CO  laser markets to focus more on custom projects
within the military and defense sectors.
After shifting to military and defense market sectors, APC experienced significant
delays due to ISO and cybersecurity certifications taking nearly one year to
complete, which included lengthy approval processes with the Company's first
Although raw material shipments have now returned to pre-pandemic levels,
management notes that the Company has made the strategic decision to source a
greater amount of products from US-based suppliers, reducing future risk of trade
As a result of the Company's various transitions and subsequent approvals
of certifications, management expects revenue to accelerate moving forward.
APC is a Florida-based corporation established in 2000 by the family of the Company’s
The Company was founded to bring an American presence into the CO2 laser optics
industry and provide more competition, product quality, and customer service when
In 2008, current president, Mr. Walters, purchased the Company from his family and joined
APC to provide manufacturing and technical skills, resulting in a redevelopment of the
Company’s manufacturing process by implementing greater automation, updated
equipment, and overall ensuring production is as cost-effective as possible without
Mr. Walters formally became the Company’s president in 2015.
Mr. Walters’ current duties include directing sales, engineering, research & development
In 2011, the entity Photonics Property, Inc. was established to hold ownership of the
Company’s facility, in addition to obtaining 100% ownership of APC.
that Photonics Property, Inc. is not expected to be included in the sale.
In 2014, Mrs. Ana Piumbini joined APC as its CEO, overseeing its revenue strategy, cost
controls, contract negotiation, sales, marketing, finance, and operations.
notes that Mrs. Piumbini began her position at APC with a focus on driving growth and
Mrs. Piumbini currently focuses on expanding the Company’s distribution channels,
diversifying its product catalog, focusing on the efficient use of its raw materials, and
continuously keeping high standards for its quality control and customer service.
The Companies have elected to be taxed as an S corporation under the
Internal Revenue Code. Accordingly, the stockholders of the Companies are taxed on their
proportionate share of the Companies' taxable income and therefore, no income taxes have
been provided for the Companies in the financial statements.
After many years in the industry, Mrs. Piumbini and Mr. Walters would like to start exploring
retirement options, and join with a partner who can capitalize on the many opportunities
Mrs. Piumbini and Mr. Walters are willing to remain with the Company for a period of time,
potentially up to a couple of years, to ensure a smooth transition under new ownership,
with management noting that Mr. Walters is open to remaining with the Company long-
APC is a provider of high-precision optical components, film coatings, and other infrared
products to support complete CO2 and fiber laser systems.
The Company provides a wide range of products for low-power and high-power CO2
optics, infrared optics, and custom optics.
The Company produces components such as lenses, mirrors, cavity optics, beam
handlers, windows, reflectors, and various accessories.
APC currently offers over 5,000 individual items to customers.
The Company differentiates itself by working with customers closely to design and
APC specializes in manufacturing replacement laser cutting parts and accessories for
APC creates components suited for a wide variety of OEM products,
including brands such as Trumpf, Amada, Bystronic, Cincinnati, LVD, Mazak, Mitsubishi,
All of the Company’s products are manufactured in-house using advanced techniques
Management notes that APC is one of three companies globally
that manufactures CO2 laser cutting products with computer numerical control (CNC)
In recent years, the Company has increased its e-commerce sales channels with sales
primarily occurring through its website, representing approximately 18% of overall
The Company offers over 30 different film coatings for its optical products, including anti-
APC maintains high-quality requirements when procuring and testing ZnSe, the base
material for all its optical products.
With the use of high-quality ZnSe, APC enjoys a competitive advantage with laser
grade products rated up to 8,000 watts.
The Company is able to manufacture complete custom projects, including unique focal
lengths, the use of soft crystals, special coatings, or entirely custom designs.
In addition to manufactured products, APC provides raw materials to other industrial
operators, such as ZnSe sheets, silicone, copper, and gallium arsenide.
APC has an engineering staff to assist in product development, including new products,
improvements, management notes that its coating technology has one of the lowest
absorption rates in the industry for both low-power and high-power uses.
APC offers after-sales services such as telephone support, online chat, and video training.
Pertains to unique projects for customers that are designed and built
Custom projects are approximately equally split between high-power and low-
APC specializes in providing lenses and other optical
components related to low & high-power CO2 engraving and cutting.
These products are designed for below 1,000 watts (low-power) and above 1,000 watts
Use cases primarily relate to sheet metal cutting, laser marking, engraving, and various
APC resells certain third-party products, primarily related to nozzles and
Includes revenue categories such as raw material sales, servicing, and
The Company sells raw materials on an individual basis, including products such as ZnSe,
zinc sulfide, silicon, gallium arsenide, and copper.
APC is able to apply 30 different film coatings, using methods such as physical vapor
deposition, chemical vapor deposition, sputtering, and evaporation.
APC offers expert optical product installation, where it utilizes an in-house clean room to
ensure assembly is scratch-free, leak-tested, and optically tested.

Management notes that, in response to evolving market demands, APC has aggressively
diversified its product portfolio, launching over 20 new products from 2020 to 2024 in the
low-power CO2 laser segment and custom optics solutions.
The ongoing innovation strategy is designed to stay ahead of industry trends and
expand APC’s footprint in high-growth markets.
Management notes there are numerous innovative products set to be launched over the
next few years, which include the following:
APC now offers high-quality ZnSe raw materials for
component manufacturers, backed by an exclusive three-year distribution agreement
with KM Innovation, which signed in January 2025, for sales in North & South America and
Designed for CO2 lasers, providing accessible solutions for
hobbyists, small businesses, and industrial applications.
A new product line focused on enhancing safety against
speed, precision, and durability, targeting industrial and creative applications.
In addition to the Company’s new product lines, management notes there are numerous
market opportunities that APC can capitalize on, and benefit from in the coming years,
By diversifying the supply sources of
Ga and Ge beyond China, the Company can focus on procuring materials from
India and South Korea to counteract current US import restrictions, mitigating risks
to APC’s operations and production costs.
A focus on establishing strategic resale affiliations will help secure a competitive
supply chain advantage in laser optics manufacturing.

A push to invest in fiber laser collimators and lenses

The affiliation will require a dedicated sales force and product

Broaden Product Diversification via Strategic Alliances:
with global suppliers to buy, resell, and co-develop advanced optics solutions across the
defense, industrial, and medical laser markets.
Management notes that the plans for growth and expansion that the Company has
identified and began working on in recent years will lead to several benefits, including a
more resilient supply chain via raw materials sourcing expansion, new revenue streams,
and a strategic market position for APC, as various affiliations and resale agreements
will allow APC to expand its customer base and technology capabilities.
With exclusive ZnSe distribution, a stronger fiber laser portfolio, and expanded sourcing
of critical raw materials, APC is positioned for long-term scalability in industrial, defense,
and high-power laser markets, according to management.
Management notes that APC maintains robust quality control and security compliance
The Company utilizes its ISO 9001:2015-certified quality management system, which
ensures precision, reliability, and compliance with industrial, medical, aerospace, and
In addition to its ISO certification, the Company also uses various forms of advanced
Measures surface precision, wavefront accuracy, and optical
Ensures critical alignment in laser optics assembly.

management notes strengthens APC's position as a trusted supplier for military,
The restricted-access security room is designed to
handle and store sensitive military blueprints, classified project specifications, and ITAR-
The Company's adherence to NIST 800-171 regulations ensures compliance with US DoD
For post-fabrication inspection & compliance testing, all finished products undergo

Ensures precision testing and contamination-free coating
Provides accurate absorption testing for high-power laser

By striving to meet ISO 9001:2015, ITAR, and NIST cybersecurity standards, the Company
can always be prepared for potential defense and military contracts.

Management notes that the Company's use of high-performance measurement tools

With its newly established military security room, ISO-
certified quality control, and advanced testing capabilities, APC is now fully positioned

APC works with customers primarily in the US (98.5%), in industries such as medical,
scientific, manufacturing, automotive, aerospace, and military, and mainly selling to
APC works with a global supply network of distributors in over 85 countries.
Management notes that distributors are typically able to purchase products from APC
at a discounted price, and sell them at market rates in markets worldwide.
Sales from distributors account for approximately 30% of overall revenue each year.
Management notes the Company sources new customers through a variety of methods,
including word-of-mouth, direct outreach through its sales team, marketing activities,
The Company sources additional customers through its presence in trade associations
The International Society for Optics and Photonics (SPIE)
APC has implemented customer retention strategies, including loyalty programs for
repeat purchases and long-term engagement, incentive programs, and constant
attention to product quality and customer support.
In recent years, the Company has found large revenue growth by expanding into e-
commerce sales channels, with the most significant being through its website, with a
lesser amount through Amazon, eBay, and Shopify.
In 2024, approximately 18% of overall revenue was generated through e-commerce
Management notes that the Company has also focused on expanding internationally,
with target markets in China, Brazil, and India.
When an order is received, APC typically has a turnaround time of two weeks to one month
before the order is shipped to the customer.
On average, the Company completes about 850 orders every month, according to
The chart below lists the total current, open orders for the Company, listing the original

APC operates from an 18,000 sq. ft. facility in Sarasota, Florida, leased from an affiliated
The Company currently leases 12,000 sq. ft. of the facility for $5,500 per month.
Management notes that the Company is operating at an approximate 30%
capacity, with additional space available for future expansion.
Management notes that the room for operational growth, without requiring
additional square footage, makes the current facility an efficient and scalable
The additional 6,000 sq. ft. of the facility is currently leased to Corvo Cup, a plastic injection
molding company, under a 12-month lease agreement.
The Company's world-class facility is designed and built to maximize efficiency and
Management notes that the Company has
incorporated state-of-the-art technology into its fabrication processes, including CNC
optical equipment, inspection equipment, and two coating labs.
APC’s coating labs are vacuum chambers that create multi-layer, anti-reflection,
reflective beam splitter, polarizer, mirror, and filter coatings.
The Company’s facility includes both a warehouse and office space.
Management notes that APC is ITAR registered and compliant with ITAR regulations during
the manufacturing, selling, and distributing of defense and space-related articles.
The current facility is leased from Photonics Property, Inc., with the owners being open to
entering into a long-term lease agreement.
Management notes that the current rent expense for the facility is below market
rate, though discussions with new ownership can be had to determine the rent
Given that management is open to discussions for the future rent expense, no
adjustment to rent has been made at this time.
APC operates with a team of approximately 22 W2
occasionally utilizes 1099 personnel on an as-needed basis for projects.
The Company offers various benefits to employees, including health, dental, and vision
insurance, 401(k) retirement plans with company match, and paid time off (PTO).
The Company’s payroll processes are outsourced to Co-Advantage.
that the Company’s entire workforce operates out of its Sarasota, FL headquarters, with

for its production processes, and certain third-party products for resale.

APC reports income and expenses on the accrual method of accounting. The Company uses
QuickBooks accounting software. The following table presents key income statement account
groups for the historical period. Management notes that the Company faced a significant
negative financial impact from the COVID-19 pandemic, particularly caused by delays in raw
material shipments from trade restrictions, halted production, and decreased demand from
delayed orders and increased costs. Although raw material shipments have now returned to
pre-pandemic levels, management notes that the Company has made the strategic decision
to source a greater amount of products from US-based suppliers, reducing future risk of trade
restrictions. As a result of the Company's various transitions and subsequent approvals of
certifications, management expects revenue to accelerate moving forward.
Management notes that during the historical periods, overall revenue
declined due to key raw material suppliers in China having trade restricted, or closing entirely,
leading to decreased demand from delayed orders and increased costs. Although raw material
shipments have now returned to pre-pandemic levels, management notes that the Company
has made the strategic decision to source a greater amount of products from US-based
suppliers, reducing future risk of trade restrictions

In 2021 and 2022, APC installed solar power systems on its facility,
and connected its septic system directly to municipal providers, leading to the increase in
Beginning in 2022, APC significantly decreased its travel
spending as a result of lingering COVID-19 effects. During the height of the pandemic, trade
shows were delayed and canceled, leading operators in this industry to develop marketing
efforts beyond in-person events. In 2021 and 2022, these events were restarted but with
significantly lower participation than pre-pandemic levels, leading APC to also limit its
APC began using QuickBooks as its accounting software in 2021, and, at
the time was recording certain payroll expenses under professional fees. This recording
practice was corrected in 2022, leading to the subsequent decrease of this line item.
The Company’s rent expense fluctuated largely due to its facility being owned by a
related entity. In 2023, APC began expensing a consistent $66k annually in rent, with
management noting this payment being below market rate, with a more appropriate level
being $216k annually for the full facility.
Primarily includes expenses such as currency exchange fees,
education & training, donations, recruitment, and employee engagement.
Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) have been adjusted
for non-transferable and non-recurring expenditures in order to better reflect the “economic
level” of earnings. These adjustments are summarized below. In addition, management notes
that there are adjustments that can be made for the current rent expense of the Company's
operating facility, as well as compensation to both Mrs. Piumbini and Mr. Walters. Given that
the owners are open to discussions regarding these potential adjustments based on deal
structure, their roles with APC moving forward, etc., there have been no adjustments made to
rent or owners' compensation at this time.

From 2024 to 2030, global revenue for this industry is expected to increase at a CAGR of
Increased demand for this industry’s products is being fueled by the desire for high-end
microscopy systems, as well as technological developments in microscopes.
Growing demand for collimating lenses, and the increasing importance of fiber optics
collimating lenses will also create opportunities for industry operators.
The automotive sector in particular has represented a large share of this industry’s
revenue, both through production methods and usage in the end-product.
The North American market accounts for the largest share of this industry’s revenue, and
with technological advances, further demand for collimation lenses is likely to increase
Although COVID-19 presented negative impacts on production and trade of products for
this industry, future demand is likely to grow as a result, due to increased attention on
medical research and the corresponding need for advanced optical lenses.
If you have the resources necessary to fund the equity portion of your acquisition and direct
access to the debt funding necessary to complete the transaction, we strongly recommend
that your next step is for Benchmark International to arrange a meeting with Laser Lens Tek,
Inc. At the meeting, you and the seller will discuss the opportunity to further evaluate if the
acquisition meets both parties’ strategic goals. Please contact Sean Fechtmann directly to
Please be prepared to provide persuasive support for the availability of the necessary funding
early in this process. This will be of high importance to our clients and failure to be convincing
on this point may lead to an early termination of your involvement in this opportunity.

IN ADDITION TO THE HISTORICAL INFORMATION CONTAINED HEREIN, THIS MEMORANDUM CONTAINS
FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS THAT INVOLVE RISK AND UNCERTAINTIES. STATEMENTS
CONTAINED IN THIS MEMORANDUM, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, STATEMENTS CONTAINING
THE WORDS “BELIEVE,” “ARE OF THE OPINION,” “ANTICIPATE,” “ESTIMATE,” “EXPECT,” AND WORDS OF
HEREIN CONSTITUTING FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS INCLUDES, BUT IS NOT LIMITED TO, THE (I)
PROJECTED FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE OF THE BUSINESS; (II) THE EXPECTED DEVELOPMENT OF THE
BUSINESS’ OPERATIONS, PROJECTS, AND JOINT VENTURES; (III) EXECUTION OF THE BUSINESS’ VISION
AND GROWTH STRATEGY; (IV) COMPLETION OF THE BUSINESS’ PROJECTS THAT ARE CURRENTLY
UNDERWAY, IN DEVELOPMENT OR OTHERWISE UNDER CONSIDERATION; AND (V) RENEWAL OF THE
BUSINESS’ CURRENT CUSTOMER, SUPPLIER AND OTHER MATERIAL AGREEMENTS. FORWARD-LOOKING
STATEMENTS ARE PROVIDED TO ALLOW RECIPIENTS THE OPPORTUNITY TO UNDERSTAND
MANAGEMENT’S BELIEFS AND OPINIONS IN RESPECT OF THE FUTURE SO THAT THEY MAY USE SUCH
BELIEFS AND OPINIONS AS ONE FACTOR IN EVALUATING AN INVESTMENT. FORWARD-LOOKING
STATEMENTS ARE NECESSARILY BASED UPON CERTAIN ESTIMATES AND ASSUMPTIONS. ANY
ASSUMPTIONS SHOULD NOT BE CONSTRUED TO BE INDICATIVE OF THE ACTUAL EVENTS THAT WILL
OCCUR. ACTUAL EVENTS ARE DIFFICULT TO PREDICT AND MAY DEPEND UPON FACTORS THAT ARE
BEYOND THE CONTROL OF THE BUSINESS AND ITS MANAGEMENT. CERTAIN ASSUMPTIONS HAVE
BEEN MADE TO SIMPLIFY THE PRESENTATION AND, ACCORDINGLY, ACTUAL RESULTS MAY DIFFER,
PERHAPS MATERIALLY, FROM THOSE PRESENTED. SOME IMPORTANT FACTORS THAT COULD CAUSE
ACTUAL RESULTS TO DIFFER MATERIALLY FROM THOSE IN ANY FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS
INCLUDE, BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO, THE FOLLOWING: FINANCIAL, MARKET, ECONOMIC,
TECHNOLOGICAL, PERSONNEL, REGULATORY, OR LEGAL CONDITIONS OR CHANGES. RECIPIENTS ARE
CAUTIONED NOT TO PLACE UNDUE RELIANCE ON FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS. RECIPIENTS
SHOULD CONDUCT THEIR OWN ANALYSIS, USING SUCH ASSUMPTIONS AS THEY DEEM APPROPRIATE,
AND SHOULD FULLY CONSIDER OTHER AVAILABLE INFORMATION. NEITHER THE BUSINESS NOR
BENCHMARK INTERNATIONAL UNDERTAKES ANY OBLIGATION TO UPDATE FORWARD-LOOKING
STATEMENTS IF CIRCUMSTANCES OR MANAGEMENT’S ESTIMATES OR OPINIONS SHOULD CHANGE.

the sale process UNLESS SPECIFICALLY AGREED TO IN WRITING. UNDER NO CIRCUMSTANCES
SHOULD RECIPIENT CONTACT OTHER EMPLOYEES OF THE BUSINESS OR VISIT ANY SITES USED
RECIPIENTS OF THIS MEMORANDUM IN JURISDICTIONS OUTSIDE THE UNITED STATES SHOULD INFORM
THEMSELVES ABOUT AND OBSERVE ALL APPLICABLE LEGAL REQUIREMENTS IN THEIR JURISDICTIONS.
IN PARTICULAR, THE DISTRIBUTION OF THIS MEMORANDUM IN CERTAIN JURISDICTIONS MAY BE
RESTRICTED BY LAW AND, ACCORDINGLY, RECIPIENTS REPRESENT THAT THEY ARE ABLE TO RECEIVE
THIS MEMORANDUM WITHOUT CONTRAVENTION OF ANY UNFULFILLED REGISTRATION REQUIREMENTS
OR OTHER LEGAL RESTRICTIONS IN THE JURISDICTION IN WHICH THEY RESIDE OR CONDUCT BUSINESS.
